message:
fix(stdlib): strtod sets errno=ERANGE on overflow/underflow + diff harness

CONFORMANCE: libc stdlib.h numeric diff matrix.

Differential harness exposed a real strtod conformance defect: glibc
sets errno=ERANGE on inputs that overflow to ±Inf ("1e400") or
underflow to 0 ("1e-400"); FrankenLibC's strtod returned the right
value (Inf / 0) but never touched errno. POSIX requires the errno
side effect for callers to distinguish overflow-Inf from a literal
"INF" input, and underflow-0 from a literal "0" input.

Fix: after parsing in the ABI wrapper, inspect the result + the
consumed prefix:
  - val.is_infinite() AND prefix has no "inf" literal → ERANGE
  - val == 0.0 AND prefix has any non-zero digit       → ERANGE

Two helpers added: contains_inf_literal and contains_nonzero_digit.

Diff harness covers 8 functions (atoi/atol/atoll/strtol/strtoll/
strtoul/strtoull/strtod) with 16-26 hand-curated edge cases each:
- leading whitespace, signs, base prefixes (0x / 0X / 0)
- empty, sign-only, whitespace-only inputs
- INT_MAX/MIN/UINT_MAX/LONG_MAX/MIN/ULLONG_MAX/DBL_MAX boundaries
- overflow + underflow (ERANGE oracle)
- trailing garbage (endptr offset)
- bare "0x" / "0xZZ" (consume only "0")
- hex floats (0x1.8p+1)
- INF / NAN literals (NaN==NaN bit equality)

Per case we compare return value, endptr offset, AND ERANGE-vs-not
errno. Non-ERANGE errno values are implementation-defined per POSIX
and not asserted.

After fix: 9/9 stdlib_numeric diff tests pass, 0 divergences.

Full abi suite: 3010/3010 pass across 61 suites (was 3001/60;
+9 from new harness).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
